# Project Desctription 
Jamie Element's project for the AI Copilot Hackathon.


## Project Guidelines
- Maintain consistent styling across all pages
- Keep file and folder names descriptive and organized
- Before generating new files, if there are not any existing files of that type, look for an appropriate standards file from https://github.com/github/awesome-copilot/tree/main/instructions and add it to our repo as a new instruction file under `.github/instructions`. This will help ensure high standards for code quality and consistency across the project.

# Documentation
- Any time you are prompted, make a note of the prompt and the response in a markdown file in the `docs/prompt-log` folder. This will help us keep track of the development process and decisions made along the way.
- Include any relevant code snippets, explanations, and reasoning behind the decisions made in response to the prompts. This documentation will be valuable for future reference and for anyone else who may work on the project in the future.
- Include a date and timestamp in the filename for each entry in the documentation to keep track of when decisions were made and when certain features were implemented. This will help us understand the timeline of the project and the evolution of our design choices.
- The file name should be in the formating of `YYYY-MM-DD-HH-MM-description.md` to ensure that the files are organized chronologically and are easy to navigate.
- When ammending existing files, make sure to update the documentation in `docs/prompt-log` to reflect the changes made and the reasons behind those changes, along with the updated prompt and response. This will help maintain a clear record of the project's development and ensure that all decisions are well-documented for future reference.
